---
title: 'Arterial Stiffness and Cognitive Decline: Interactive Visualizations'
author: "Yuyao Zhang"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
runtime: shiny
resource_files:
- ehbs_20101014.sas7bdat
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
```

```{r load-packages}
library(haven)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(plotly)
library(survival)
library(broom)
library(tidyr)
library(shiny)
```

```{r load-and-prepare-data}
# Load raw data
df <- read_sas("ehbs_20101014.sas7bdat")

# Recode variables
df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    sex       = factor(sex,       levels = c(0, 1),    labels = c("Female", "Male")),
    diabetes  = factor(diabetes_baseline, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("No", "Yes")),
    race      = factor(race,      levels = c(0, 1, 2), labels = c("White", "Black", "Other")),
    education = factor(education, levels = c(0, 1, 2),
                       labels = c("Less than college", "College degree", "More than college"))
  ) %>%
  filter(moca_baseline <= 30 | is.na(moca_baseline))

# Build survival dataset
df_long <- df %>%
  select(ehbs_id, sygmacor_pulse_wave, age_baseline, sex, race, education,
         bmi, sygmacor_systolic, sygmacor_diastolic, diabetes,
         moca_baseline, moca_yr3, moca_yr5, moca_yr7, moca_yr9,
         time_yr3, time_yr5, time_yr7, time_yr9) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols         = c(moca_baseline, moca_yr3, moca_yr5, moca_yr7, moca_yr9),
    names_to     = "visit",
    values_to    = "moca_score",
    names_prefix = "moca_"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    time_days  = case_when(
      visit == "baseline" ~ 0,
      visit == "yr3"      ~ time_yr3,
      visit == "yr5"      ~ time_yr5,
      visit == "yr7"      ~ time_yr7,
      visit == "yr9"      ~ time_yr9
    ),
    time_years = time_days / 365.25
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(moca_score) & moca_score <= 30)

surv_data_full <- df_long %>%
  group_by(ehbs_id) %>%
  filter(
    any(visit == "baseline" & !is.na(moca_score) & moca_score >= 26),
    any(visit != "baseline")
  ) %>%
  arrange(ehbs_id, time_days) %>%
  summarise(
    event               = any(moca_score < 26 & visit != "baseline", na.rm = TRUE),
    time_to_event_days  = if_else(
      any(moca_score < 26 & visit != "baseline", na.rm = TRUE),
      min(time_days[moca_score < 26 & visit != "baseline"], na.rm = TRUE) - first(time_days[visit == "baseline"]),
      max(time_days, na.rm = TRUE) - first(time_days[visit == "baseline"])
    ),
    time_to_event_years = time_to_event_days / 365.25,
    age_baseline        = first(age_baseline),
    sex                 = first(sex),
    race                = first(race),
    education           = first(education),
    bmi                 = first(bmi),
    sygmacor_pulse_wave = first(sygmacor_pulse_wave),
    sygmacor_systolic   = first(sygmacor_systolic),
    sygmacor_diastolic  = first(sygmacor_diastolic),
    diabetes            = first(diabetes),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    pwv_quartile = ntile(sygmacor_pulse_wave, 4),
    pwv_group    = factor(pwv_quartile, levels = 1:4,
                          labels = c("Q1 – Lowest", "Q2 – Low",
                                     "Q3 – High",   "Q4 – Highest"))
  )

# Shared color palette
pwv_colors <- c(
  "Q1 – Lowest"  = "#2166AC",
  "Q2 – Low"     = "#74ADD1",
  "Q3 – High"    = "#F4A582",
  "Q4 – Highest" = "#D6604D"
)

# At-risk table helper
# Returns number at risk at each time break for each PWV group
get_at_risk <- function(surv_data, time_breaks = c(0, 2, 4, 6, 8)) {
  groups <- levels(surv_data$pwv_group)
  result <- lapply(groups, function(g) {
    d <- surv_data %>% filter(pwv_group == g)
    n_at_risk <- sapply(time_breaks, function(t) {
      sum(d$time_to_event_years >= t, na.rm = TRUE)
    })
    data.frame(
      Group     = g,
      t(n_at_risk),
      check.names = FALSE
    )
  })
  tbl <- do.call(rbind, result)
  colnames(tbl) <- c("Group", paste0("t=", time_breaks))
  tbl
}
```

---

```{r dashboard}
shinyApp(
  ui = fluidPage(

    # Global CSS
    tags$head(tags$style(HTML("
      body { font-family: Arial, sans-serif; background: #f4f6f9; }

      .dashboard-header {
        background: linear-gradient(135deg, #1F3864 0%, #2E75B6 100%);
        color: white;
        padding: 18px 28px 14px 28px;
        margin-bottom: 18px;
        border-radius: 6px;
      }
      .dashboard-header h2 { margin: 0 0 4px 0; font-size: 22px; }
      .dashboard-header p  { margin: 0; font-size: 13px; opacity: 0.85; }

      .sidebar-panel {
        background: #ffffff;
        border: 1px solid #dde3ec;
        border-radius: 6px;
        padding: 16px;
      }
      .sidebar-panel h4 {
        color: #2E75B6;
        margin-top: 0;
        font-size: 14px;
        border-bottom: 1px solid #dde3ec;
        padding-bottom: 8px;
      }

      .plot-card {
        background: white;
        border: 1px solid #dde3ec;
        border-radius: 6px;
        padding: 16px;
        margin-bottom: 14px;
      }
      .plot-card h4 {
        color: #1F3864;
        font-size: 15px;
        margin-top: 0;
        margin-bottom: 4px;
      }
      .plot-card .subtitle {
        color: #777;
        font-size: 12px;
        margin-bottom: 12px;
      }

      .takeaway-box {
        background: #EBF3FB;
        border-left: 4px solid #2E75B6;
        padding: 10px 14px;
        border-radius: 0 4px 4px 0;
        font-size: 12.5px;
        color: #333;
        margin-top: 10px;
      }

      /* At-risk table */
      .risk-table-wrap {
        margin-top: 6px;
        overflow-x: auto;
      }
      .risk-table {
        width: 100%;
        font-size: 12px;
        border-collapse: collapse;
        font-family: monospace;
      }
      .risk-table th {
        background: #f0f4f8;
        padding: 5px 10px;
        text-align: center;
        border: 1px solid #dde3ec;
        font-weight: bold;
        color: #444;
      }
      .risk-table td {
        padding: 4px 10px;
        text-align: center;
        border: 1px solid #dde3ec;
      }
      .risk-table td:first-child {
        text-align: left;
        font-weight: bold;
        white-space: nowrap;
      }

      .stat-badge {
        display: inline-block;
        background: #EBF3FB;
        border: 1px solid #b3d1ee;
        border-radius: 4px;
        padding: 4px 10px;
        font-size: 13px;
        color: #1F3864;
        font-weight: bold;
        margin-top: 6px;
      }

      .tab-content { padding-top: 10px; }

      /* Mobile responsiveness */
      @media (max-width: 768px) {
        .col-sm-3, .col-sm-9 {
          width: 100% !important;
          float: none !important;
        }
        .dashboard-header h2 { font-size: 17px; }
        .plot-card { padding: 10px; }
        .stat-badge { font-size: 12px; }
      }
    "))),

    # Header
    div(class = "dashboard-header",
      tags$h2("Arterial Stiffness and Cognitive Decline"),
      tags$p("Explore how baseline pulse wave velocity (PWV) relates to cognitive
              performance and time to cognitive impairment. Filter by demographic
              subgroup to examine consistency of associations.")
    ),

    # Main layout: sidebar + tabbed plots
    fluidRow(

      # Sidebar (shared filters)
      column(3,
        div(class = "sidebar-panel",
          h4("Filter Participants"),

          checkboxGroupInput("sex_filter", "Sex:",
                             choices  = c("Female", "Male"),
                             selected = c("Female", "Male")),

          checkboxGroupInput("race_filter", "Race / Ethnicity:",
                             choices  = c("White", "Black", "Other"),
                             selected = c("White", "Black", "Other")),

          checkboxGroupInput("edu_filter", "Education:",
                             choices  = c("Less than college",
                                          "College degree",
                                          "More than college"),
                             selected = c("Less than college",
                                          "College degree",
                                          "More than college")),
          hr(),
          uiOutput("n_badge"),
          uiOutput("pval_badge")
        )
      ),

      # Tabbed plots
      column(9,
        tabsetPanel(
          type = "tabs",

          # Tab 1: Scatter
          tabPanel("Baseline Association",
            div(class = "plot-card",
              h4("Baseline Pulse Wave Velocity vs. Cognitive Performance"),
              div(class = "subtitle",
                  "Cross-sectional relationship at study enrollment"),
              plotlyOutput("scatter_plot", height = "400px"),
              div(class = "takeaway-box",
                tags$b("Key Takeaway: "),
                "Higher baseline PWV is associated with lower MoCA scores,
                 as shown by the downward-sloping trend line. Color indicates
                 PWV quartile. Participants in Q4 (red-orange) tend to cluster
                 at lower cognitive scores compared to Q1 (blue). This pattern
                 persists across demographic subgroups."
              )
            )
          ),

          # Tab 2: KM curve
          tabPanel("Survival Analysis",
            div(class = "plot-card",
              h4("Time to Cognitive Impairment by PWV Quartile"),
              div(class = "subtitle",
                  "Kaplan-Meier curves: probability of remaining cognitively impairment-free"),
              plotlyOutput("km_plot", height = "380px"),
              div(class = "risk-table-wrap",
                tags$p(tags$b("Number at risk"),
                       style = "font-size:12px; color:#444; margin:10px 0 4px 0;"),
                tableOutput("risk_table")
              ),
              div(class = "takeaway-box",
                tags$b("Key Takeaway: "),
                "Participants with the highest PWV (Q4) experienced cognitive
                 impairment significantly earlier than those with the lowest PWV (Q1).
                 The number-at-risk table below the curves shows how many participants
                 in each quartile remained event-free at each follow-up time point.
                 The log-rank p-value (sidebar) updates as filters are applied."
              )
            )
          ),

          # Tab 3: About the Data
          tabPanel("About the Data",
            div(style = "padding: 20px; max-width: 800px;",
              h4(style = "color:#1F3864; margin-bottom:16px;",
                 "Dataset Description"),
              p(style = "font-size:14px; line-height:1.8; color:#333;",
                "This dashboard uses data from the Emory Healthy Brain Study (EHBS),
                 a prospective community-based cohort study nested within the Emory Healthy
                 Aging Study (EHAS), enrolling adults aged 50–75 years who were cognitively
                 normal at baseline, across the United States. The dataset is confidential
                 and not publicly available. Data were collected through in-person biennial
                 assessments including cardiovascular measures (pulse wave velocity),
                 neuropsychological testing (MoCA), neuroimaging, and biospecimen collection.
                 A total of 1,822 participants were enrolled in the overall study; the analytic
                 sample for this dashboard includes participants with available baseline PWV and
                 MoCA scores, with those having cognitive impairment at baseline (MoCA < 26)
                 excluded from survival analyses to assess incident cognitive impairment."
              ),
              hr(),
              div(style = "background:#EBF3FB; border-left:4px solid #2E75B6;
                           padding:10px 14px; border-radius:0 4px 4px 0;
                           font-size:13px; color:#333; margin-top:10px;",
                tags$b("Real-World Impact: "),
                "Arterial stiffness, measured by pulse wave velocity, is a modifiable
                 cardiovascular risk factor that may serve as an early marker for cognitive
                 decline before clinical symptoms appear. Identifying this association can
                 inform preventive strategies targeting vascular health to reduce the burden
                 of dementia."
              )
            )
          )

        )   # end tabsetPanel
      )     # end column(9)
    )       # end fluidRow (main layout)
  ),        # end fluidPage / end ui

  # Server
  server = function(input, output, session) {

    # Reactive: filtered scatter data
    scatter_data <- reactive({
      df %>%
        filter(
          !is.na(sygmacor_pulse_wave),
          !is.na(moca_baseline),
          sex       %in% input$sex_filter,
          race      %in% input$race_filter,
          education %in% input$edu_filter
        ) %>%
        mutate(
          pwv_quartile = ntile(sygmacor_pulse_wave, 4),
          pwv_group    = factor(pwv_quartile, levels = 1:4,
                                labels = c("Q1 – Lowest", "Q2 – Low",
                                           "Q3 – High",   "Q4 – Highest"))
        )
    })

    # Reactive: filtered survival data
    surv_filtered <- reactive({
      surv_data_full %>%
        filter(
          sex       %in% input$sex_filter,
          race      %in% input$race_filter,
          education %in% input$edu_filter
        ) %>%
        mutate(
          pwv_quartile = ntile(sygmacor_pulse_wave, 4),
          pwv_group    = factor(pwv_quartile, levels = 1:4,
                                labels = c("Q1 – Lowest", "Q2 – Low",
                                           "Q3 – High",   "Q4 – Highest"))
        )
    })

    # Sidebar badges
    output$n_badge <- renderUI({
      div(class = "stat-badge", paste0("n = ", nrow(surv_filtered()), " participants"))
    })

    output$pval_badge <- renderUI({
      d <- surv_filtered()
      req(nrow(d) > 20)
      lr  <- survdiff(Surv(time_to_event_years, event) ~ pwv_group, data = d)
      p   <- 1 - pchisq(lr$chisq, df = length(lr$n) - 1)
      lbl <- if (p < 0.001) "< 0.001" else as.character(round(p, 3))
      div(class = "stat-badge",
          style = "margin-top:6px;",
          paste0("Log-rank p ", lbl))
    })

    # Widget 1: Scatter plot
    output$scatter_plot <- renderPlotly({
      d <- scatter_data()
      validate(need(nrow(d) > 10, "Please select at least one option per filter."))

      # Group-level summary for tooltip
      group_summary <- d %>%
        group_by(pwv_group) %>%
        summarise(
          n            = n(),
          median_pwv   = round(median(sygmacor_pulse_wave, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
          median_moca  = round(median(moca_baseline, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
          .groups = "drop"
        ) %>%
        mutate(tooltip = paste0(
          "<b>", pwv_group, "</b><br>",
          "N: ", n, "<br>",
          "Median PWV: ", median_pwv, " m/s<br>",
          "Median MoCA: ", median_moca
        ))

      d <- d %>% left_join(group_summary %>% select(pwv_group, tooltip),
                           by = "pwv_group")

      p <- ggplot(d, aes(x = sygmacor_pulse_wave, y = moca_baseline,
                         color = pwv_group, text = tooltip)) +
        geom_jitter(alpha = 0.45, size = 1.6, width = 0.15, height = 0.15) +
        geom_smooth(aes(group = 1), method = "lm", se = TRUE,
                    color = "black", linewidth = 0.9, linetype = "dashed",
                    fill = "grey75", alpha = 0.2) +
        scale_color_manual(values = pwv_colors, name = "PWV Quartile") +
        scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(2, 18, by = 2)) +
        scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(18, 30, by = 2)) +
        labs(
          x = "Baseline Pulse Wave Velocity (m/s)",
          y = "Baseline MoCA Score (0–30)"
        ) +
        theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
        theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank())

      ggplotly(p, tooltip = "text") %>%
        layout(legend = list(title = list(text = "<b>PWV Quartile</b>")),
               hoverlabel = list(bgcolor = "white", font = list(size = 12))) %>%
        config(displayModeBar = TRUE,
               modeBarButtonsToRemove = c("lasso2d", "select2d",
                                           "hoverClosestCartesian",
                                           "hoverCompareCartesian"))
    })

    # Widget 2: KM plot
    output$km_plot <- renderPlotly({
      d <- surv_filtered()
      validate(need(nrow(d) > 20, "Please select at least one option per filter."))

      km_fit <- survfit(Surv(time_to_event_years, event) ~ pwv_group, data = d)
      km_df  <- tidy(km_fit) %>%
        mutate(
          strata = gsub("pwv_group=", "", strata),
          strata = factor(strata, levels = c("Q1 – Lowest", "Q2 – Low",
                                              "Q3 – High",   "Q4 – Highest"))
        )

      fig <- plot_ly()

      for (grp in levels(km_df$strata)) {
        g   <- km_df %>% filter(strata == grp)
        col <- pwv_colors[grp]

        fig <- fig %>%
          add_ribbons(
            data       = g,
            x          = ~time,
            ymin       = ~conf.low,
            ymax       = ~conf.high,
            fillcolor  = adjustcolor(col, alpha.f = 0.13),
            line       = list(color = "transparent"),
            showlegend = FALSE,
            hoverinfo  = "skip",
            name       = paste0(grp, "_ci")
          ) %>%
          add_lines(
            data      = g,
            x         = ~time,
            y         = ~estimate,
            name      = grp,
            line      = list(color = col, width = 2.5, shape = "hv"),
            text      = ~paste0("<b>", strata, "</b><br>",
                                "Time: ", round(time, 1), " years<br>",
                                "P(CI-free): ", round(estimate, 3)),
            hoverinfo = "text"
          )
      }

      fig %>%
        layout(
          xaxis = list(title     = "Time (years)",
                       range     = c(0, 9), dtick = 2,
                       tickfont  = list(size = 12),
                       titlefont = list(size = 13)),
          yaxis = list(title     = "Probability of Remaining Cognitively Impairment-Free",
                       range     = c(0, 1.05), dtick = 0.25,
                       tickfont  = list(size = 12),
                       titlefont = list(size = 13)),
          legend    = list(title = list(text = "<b>PWV Quartile</b>"),
                           font  = list(size = 12)),
          hovermode = "x unified",
          margin    = list(b = 40)
        ) %>%
        config(displayModeBar = TRUE,
               modeBarButtonsToRemove = c("lasso2d", "select2d"))
    })

    # At-risk table (updates with filters)
    output$risk_table <- renderTable({
      d <- surv_filtered()
      req(nrow(d) > 20)

      time_breaks <- c(0, 2, 4, 6, 8)
      groups      <- levels(d$pwv_group)

      tbl <- lapply(groups, function(g) {
        grp_d     <- d %>% filter(pwv_group == g)
        n_at_risk <- sapply(time_breaks, function(t) {
          sum(grp_d$time_to_event_years >= t, na.rm = TRUE)
        })
        as.data.frame(t(c(n_at_risk)), col.names = paste0("Year ", time_breaks))
      })

      result           <- do.call(rbind, tbl)
      colnames(result) <- paste0("Year ", time_breaks)
      result           <- cbind("PWV Quartile" = groups, result)
      result
    },
    striped   = TRUE,
    bordered  = TRUE,
    hover     = TRUE,
    width     = "100%",
    spacing   = "s"
    )
  },

  options = list(height = 780)
)

# Real-world impact footer
fluidRow(
  column(12,
    div(style = "margin-top:18px; padding:12px 20px;
                 background:#1F3864; border-radius:6px;
                 color:white; font-size:13px; text-align:center;",
      tags$b("Why This Matters: "),
      "Arterial stiffness is a modifiable cardiovascular risk factor that may
       serve as an early marker for cognitive decline before clinical symptoms appear.
       Identifying this association can inform preventive strategies targeting
       vascular health to reduce the burden of dementia.",
      tags$span(style = "margin-left:16px;"),
      tags$a(href = "https://github.com/你的用户名/你的repo名",
             target = "_blank",
             style = "color:#BDD7EE;",
             "View Source Code on GitHub")
    )
  )
)
```
